message.content: acquaint

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

A bulleted list of the in-progress task's development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)

   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah

- 0.42.0-6 clone --scope [72]
- 0.42.0-7 Single(_) dogfood validation [72]
- 0.42.0 close-out [72]

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Lands in the 0.42.0 cycle alongside the enum
   refactor; state machine becomes scope-aware (single-
   side path skips `commit-claude`/bookmark-claude/
   `finalize-claude`; `Single(_)` is single-repo mode).
   [57],[60],[71],[72]
1. vc-x1 clone: `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` flag.
   Parallel to `init --scope`; single-repo clone target
   via the path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71],[72]
1. vc-x1 validate-desc / fix-desc:
   `--scope=code|bot|code,bot` flag. Same role vocabulary
   as elsewhere — `code` validates code's commits against
   bot, `bot` reverses, `code,bot` does both (new
   default). `Single(_)` errors here (validate compares
   two repos by definition). 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71],[72]
1. CommonArgs sweep — add `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`
   to `chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show` in one cycle (single
   shared `CommonArgs` change picks all four up). Drops
   the existing `-R`/`--repo` repeatable flag in favor of
   the new path form. 0.42.0 cycle. [60],[71],[72]
1. Help layout: force over-under everywhere. Apply
   `next_line_help(true)` at the root (or via the existing
   `cli_with_banner` walker) so every subcommand's `-h` /
   `--help` uses the same layout. Today clap auto-picks
   per-command based on the widest flag spec, so
   `sync -h` is two-column but `init -h` is over-under —
   visual inconsistency.
1. Consider renaming the `.vc-config.toml` `[workspace]`
   section. Rust readers expect `[workspace]` to mean a
   Cargo workspace, which a vc-x1 dual-repo isn't.
   Candidates: `[repo-list]`, `[project]`, `[dual-repo]`.
   Breaking change — needs migration story (read both
   names during a transition cycle, or one-shot rewrite
   in `vc-x1 sync`/`init` on first contact). Drives the
   broader "stop saying workspace in user-facing surfaces"
   sweep.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. This is
   natural home for the working-copy signal called out and
   it needs to include remotes, like remotes/origin/main. [54].
1. `vc-x1 init --dry-run` should bypass the
   `--repo-remote` path-existence preflight (currently fires
   before the dry-run early-return; observed dogfooding
   2026-04-24).
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `validate-repo` subcommand: diagnostic that runs all
   `verify_*` checks (tracking, push state freshness, ochid
   integrity, conflicts, config sanity, working-copy state)
   and reports per-check pass/fail. Exit code = number of
   failed checks. Implementation: promote
   `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity` from
   push.rs to `common.rs`. [69]
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

1	# Chores-07.md
2	
3	General chores notes — design captures (forward-looking) and
4	post-implementation chore entries. Same shape as chores-01..06.md;
5	07 starts here only because 06 grew large during the 0.40.0 /
6	0.41.0 cycles and a new top-level cycle is a natural file
7	boundary.
8	
9	Subsection headers use the trailing-version format from CLAUDE.md
10	when they correspond to a release: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
11	Pre-implementation design captures may use a plain title; once
12	implemented, the title can become a release-versioned chore.
13	
14	## --scope enum refactor (0.42.0)
15	
16	Picks up the work the 0.41.0 cycle redirected away from. The
17	foundation captured in [2] (chores-06's `### 0.41.0-4: capture
18	--scope enum vocabulary`) lands here as code: the `Scope`
19	type becomes a sum (`Roles(Vec<Side>) | Single(PathBuf)`), the
20	flag surface unifies under `--scope` / `-s` with both keyword and
21	path forms, and every dual-repo-aware command migrates to the
22	new shape in dependency order.
23	
24	**Cycle steps (initial sketch — sub-step boundaries can shift
25	once each command's call sites are seen).**
26	
27	- **0.42.0-0** — this plan + version bump + new
28	  `notes/chores-07.md`. Notes only.
29	- **0.42.0-1** — `scope.rs` enum. Internal only — no
30	  CLI changes yet, no consumers updated. Existing
31	  `Scope(Vec<Side>)` → `enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>),
32	  Single(PathBuf) }`. Helpers (`has_code`, `has_bot`,
33	  `is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_empty`) shift to operate
34	  on `Roles` only; `Single(_)` is a distinct mode. Tests
35	  follow.
36	- **0.42.0-2** — custom CLI value parser for `--scope` +
37	  retrofit `init`. Parser handles the keyword set
38	  (`code|bot|code,bot|bot,code`) and the prefixed-path
39	  form (`./...`, `/...`, `~/...`). Bare names that aren't
40	  keywords error with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.
41	  Init is the smallest consumer (it already takes
42	  `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`); migrating it first proves
43	  the parser end-to-end before sync's more complex
44	  resolution logic comes onto the new shape.
45	- **0.42.0-3** — retrofit `sync`. Drop `-R/--repo`. Add
46	  `-s` short form. Default-scope rules update to the
47	  three-state model from [2]: dual workspace →
48	  `Roles([Code, Bot])`, single-repo workspace →
49	  `Roles([Code])`, POR → `Single(cwd)`. `scope_to_repos`
50	  in `common.rs` updates to handle the `Single(_)`
51	  variant. Update sync's integration tests where they
52	  exercise the resolver chain.
53	- **0.42.0-4** — `push --scope`. State machine becomes
54	  scope-aware: each stage either runs or no-ops based on
55	  scope. `Single(<path>)` mode means single-repo push
56	  (no `commit-claude` / `bookmark-claude` /
57	  `finalize-claude`, no `ochid:` trailer). `Roles(...)`
58	  modes work as captured in chores-06 [1]. Persist scope
59	  in `PushState` so resumes use the same scope. Add
60	  integration tests for each scope shape.
61	- **0.42.0-5** — `finalize --scope`. Replace the existing
62	  `--repo` flag with `--scope` (`Roles` for the dual case,
63	  `Single(<path>)` for the single-repo case). `--repo`
64	  goes away; per the 0.41.0-3 capture this is the
65	  intentional break, not a deprecation.
66	- **0.42.0-6** — `clone --scope`. Parallel to init —
67	  bootstrap with the appropriate scope. Single-repo clone
68	  via the path form for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
69	- **0.42.0-7** — Single(_) dogfood validation. Apply the
70	  full `sync → edit → push` flow against a fresh POR or
71	  single-repo fixture (likely the `vc-template-x1` repo
72	  itself, since it's the canonical single-repo target).
73	  Surface and fix anything the unit tests miss.
74	- **0.42.0 (final)** — cycle close-out. Drops the
75	  `-N` suffix; updates `notes/todo.md`'s In Progress and
76	  Done sections; chores-07 close-out subsection.
77	
78	**Deferred to later cycles (per [2]).**
79	
80	- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`. Read-side
81	  commands; `Single(_)` errors there (validate compares
82	  two repos by definition).
83	- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — CommonArgs sweep.
84	  All four pick up `--scope` via a shared change to
85	  `CommonArgs`; existing `-R/--repo` retires there too.
86	- `--message-file PATH` for push. Independent push
87	  feature; gates the CLAUDE.md refresh from CLAUDE2.
88	
89	**References.** [2] points back at chores-06's
90	`### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope enum vocabulary` for
91	the full vocabulary, type-model, and per-command
92	applicability matrix. Read that subsection first before
93	diving into any of the `-N` steps below.
94	
95	### 0.42.0-1: scope.rs enum
96	
97	Internal-only refactor: tuple-struct `Scope(Vec<Side>)` →
98	`enum Scope { Roles(Vec<Side>), Single(PathBuf) }`. No CLI
99	surface changes, no consumer logic changes — every existing
100	caller still constructs and consumes `Roles(_)`; `Single(_)`
101	is staged for the parser (-2) and the sync resolver (-3).
102	
103	- `src/scope.rs`: enum body + `PathBuf` import; helpers
104	  (`has_code` / `has_bot` / `is_*_only` / `is_both` /
105	  `is_empty`) reflect the `Roles` arm via `matches!`,
106	  returning `false` (resp. not-empty) on `Single(_)`. New
107	  test pins the `Single(_)` helper behavior; existing
108	  Roles tests retitled accordingly. `#[allow(dead_code)]`
109	  on the `Single` variant for the staging window.
110	- `src/common.rs`: `default_scope` returns
111	  `Scope::Roles(_)`; `scope_to_repos` matches on the enum
112	  and returns an explicit error for `Single(_)` carrying
113	  the `0.42.0-3` staging marker. New test locks that
114	  contract so the -3 wire-up is forced to update it.
115	- `src/sync.rs`: `Some(sides) => Scope::Roles(sides.clone())`
116	  in `resolve_args_to_repos`; integration-test
117	  constructors switch to `Scope::Roles(...)`.
118	- `src/init.rs`: `plan_init` constructs `Scope::Roles(...)`
119	  for both the default and explicit-flag paths. Helper
120	  call sites (`is_code_only`, `is_both`, `is_bot_only`)
121	  unchanged — the methods continue to work on `Roles`.
122	
123	Build is warning-clean; 257 tests pass (255 baseline + 2
124	new in scope/common).
125	
126	### 0.42.0-2: --scope parser + retrofit init
127	
128	Lands the custom CLI value parser and migrates `init` onto
129	the new `Scope` shape. Parser is the shared boundary every
130	remaining `--scope` consumer (sync, push, finalize, clone)
131	will route through in -3+; init is the smallest first
132	consumer and exercises the keyword path end-to-end. The
133	path form is accepted by the parser but rejected at init
134	with a hint pointing at `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`
135	(init creates a workspace, not a single-repo project).
136	
137	- `src/scope.rs`: `pub fn parse_scope(s: &str) -> Result<Scope, String>`.
138	  Accepts the four keyword forms (`code`, `bot`, `code,bot`,
139	  `bot,code`) plus path forms prefixed by `./`, `../`, `/`,
140	  `~/`, or the bare `~`. Anything else errors with a hint
141	  pointing at the prefix-disambiguation rule. Order is
142	  preserved on the keyword forms. Drops the now-dead
143	  `is_empty` helper (parser rejects empty input upstream;
144	  the only consumer that called it was init's redundant
145	  validation). 13 new parser unit tests.
146	- `src/init.rs`: `--scope` field flips from
147	  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (clap `value_delimiter = ','`) to
148	  `Option<Scope>` with `value_parser = crate::scope::parse_scope`.
149	  `plan_init` matches on the parsed `Scope`: `Roles(_)`
150	  takes the existing bot-only-fatal path; `Single(_)` is
151	  rejected with the init-specific hint. Test scaffolding
152	  (`fixture_scoped`) and 5 fixture call sites migrated.
153	  `scope_parses_*` tests retitled around the new field
154	  type. New `scope_path_form_rejected_at_init` test pins
155	  the rejection contract.
156	
157	Smoke-tested end-to-end via CLI:
158	
159	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code --dry-run` → succeeds
160	  (single-repo dry-run plan).
161	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope code,bot --dry-run` → succeeds.
162	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope bot --dry-run` → bot-only fatal
163	  (existing `plan_init` check).
164	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope ./foo --dry-run` → path-form
165	  fatal (new `plan_init` check).
166	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope '~/work' --dry-run` → path-form
167	  fatal.
168	- `vc-x1 init tf1 --scope foo --dry-run` → clap-level
169	  parser error with hint about `./foo` disambiguation.
170	
171	### 0.42.0-3: sync --scope retrofit
172	
173	Migrates `sync` onto the new `Scope` shape and lands the
174	`Single(_)` resolver wiring. `-R/--repo` retires (single-
175	repo callers move to `--scope=<path>`); `-s` becomes the
176	short form of `--scope`. Default-scope rules pick up the
177	three-state model captured in chores-06's [1] (-4
178	vocabulary): dual workspace → `Roles([Code, Bot])`,
179	single-repo workspace → `Roles([Code])`, POR →
180	`Single(cwd)`.
181	
182	- `src/common.rs`: `default_scope` signature gains a `cwd`
183	  parameter; the POR branch (no workspace_root) now
184	  returns `Scope::Single(cwd.to_path_buf())` instead of
185	  `Roles([Code])`. `scope_to_repos`'s `Single(p)` arm
186	  resolves to `vec![p.clone()]` (no workspace lookup;
187	  shell-style expansion happens at the parser/consumer
188	  boundary). The `0.42.0-3` staging-error test is
189	  replaced with `scope_to_repos_single_returns_path` —
190	  the happy-path contract — and `default_scope_por`
191	  becomes `default_scope_por_returns_single_cwd`.
192	- `src/sync.rs`: `--repo`/`-R` flag and the `repos:
193	  Vec<PathBuf>` field deleted. `--scope` retypes from
194	  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (with `value_delimiter = ','`) to
195	  `Option<Scope>` (with `value_parser = parse_scope`)
196	  and gains `short = 's'`. SyncArgs doc rewritten around
197	  the new resolution rules. `resolve_args_to_repos`
198	  drops the `args.repos` branch and now feeds `cwd` into
199	  `default_scope`. `split_repos` and its 4 unit tests
200	  go away with `-R`; `parse_scope_repo_conflict`,
201	  `parse_single_repo_flag`, `parse_repeated_repo_flag`
202	  too. `parse_scope_*` tests assert against `Some(Scope)`;
203	  new `parse_scope_path_form` and `parse_scope_short_form`
204	  tests pin the new entry points. `apply_args` integration
205	  helper drops the `repos` field. `Side` import is gated
206	  `#[cfg(test)]` (production sync no longer needs it
207	  directly — only the tests construct `Side::*`).
208	
209	Smoke-tested end-to-end:
210	
211	- `vc-x1 sync --check` in this dual workspace → `2 repos,
212	  all bookmarks up-to-date`.
213	- `vc-x1 sync --check -s code` → `1 repo, all bookmarks
214	  up-to-date`.
215	- `vc-x1 sync --check -s bot` → ditto for `.claude`.
216	- `vc-x1 sync --check -s ./.claude` → also `1 repo` —
217	  Single-mode resolves directly to that path, bypassing
218	  the workspace's other-repo lookup.
219	- `cd /tmp && vc-x1 sync --check` → POR detected,
220	  attempts to sync `/tmp` (errors because `/tmp` isn't a
221	  jj repo — confirms `default_scope` returned
222	  `Single(cwd)`).
223	
224	# References
225	
226	[1]: /notes/chores-06.md#--scope-continuation-0410
227	[2]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-enum-vocabulary
228

  becomes `default_scope_por_returns_single_cwd`.
- `src/sync.rs`: `--repo`/`-R` flag and the `repos:
  Vec<PathBuf>` field deleted. `--scope` retypes from
  `Option<Vec<Side>>` (with `value_delimiter = ','`) to
  `Option<Scope>` (with `value_parser = parse_scope`)
  and gains `short = 's'`. SyncArgs doc rewritten around
  the new resolution rules. `resolve_args_to_repos`
  drops the `args.repos` branch and now feeds `cwd` into
  `default_scope`. `split_repos` and its 4 unit tests
  go away with `-R`; `parse_scope_repo_conflict`,
  `parse_single_repo_flag`, `parse_repeated_repo_flag`
  too. `parse_scope_*` tests assert against `Some(Scope)`;
  new `parse_scope_path_form` and `parse_scope_short_form`
  tests pin the new entry points. `apply_args` integration
  helper drops the `repos` field. `Side` import is gated
  `#[cfg(test)]` (production sync no longer needs it
  directly — only the tests construct `Side::*`).

Smoke-tested end-to-end:

- `vc-x1 sync --check` in this dual workspace → `2 repos,
  all bookmarks up-to-date`.
- `vc-x1 sync --check -s code` → `1 repo, all bookmarks
  up-to-date`.
- `vc-x1 sync --check -s bot` → ditto for `.claude`.
- `vc-x1 sync --check -s ./.claude` → also `1 repo` —
  Single-mode resolves directly to that path, bypassing
  the workspace's other-repo lookup.
- `cd /tmp && vc-x1 sync --check` → POR detected,
  attempts to sync `/tmp` (errors because `/tmp` isn't a
  jj repo — confirms `default_scope` returned
  `Single(cwd)`).

[2]: /notes/chores-06.md#0410-4-capture---scope-enum-vocabulary

content.content:
## Generalize --scope to all commands (design)

Surfaced 2026-04-23 (from the user, top of todo): vc-x1 commands
should support both single and dual repo modes. The technique
sketched for `vc-x1 push` (`--scope=app|claude|both`) is the
right shape, but the scope concept should be a project-wide
convention rather than push-only.

**Naming:** `--scope=app|other|both` — "other" rather than
"claude" because the second repo isn't always `.claude`; could
be a different bot or a different sibling project structure.

**Why scope-first** (before adding more commands or push
features): each new command that operates across both repos
would otherwise need its own ad-hoc handling. Locking in the
convention now means new commands inherit it for free, and
existing commands (sync, finalize, push) get retrofitted under
the same vocabulary.

**Rough scope of the change:**

- Shared CLI flag definition + parsing helper (likely in
  `common.rs`).
- `sync`: already accepts `-R` for single-repo. Migrate to
  `--scope`; alias or deprecate `-R` (TBD below).
- `push`: `--scope` per the existing design, with
  warn-on-WC-mismatch.
- `finalize`: already takes `--repo`; rationalize naming.
- New commands (`status` etc.) start with `--scope` from day 1.

**Open:** do we deprecate `-R` immediately, alias it, or keep
both indefinitely? Probably: alias for one minor cycle,
deprecate, remove. Decide before implementation.

## Push hardening: state + stage sanity (design)

Two related correctness fixes for `vc-x1 push` resume behavior.
Both surfaced 2026-04-22 from a 0.37.1 dogfood incident and are
described together because they tackle the same failure mode
(silent false success after stale state).

**The incident:** 0.37.1 push errored at finalize-claude;
out-of-band recovery (manual finalize + force-push of a squash)
moved the world forward; 0.37.2 push then resumed at the parked
finalize-claude, no-op'd, and falsely declared "completed all
stages" while working copies still held uncommitted changes.

**State-sanity preflight on resume.** Before any stage runs,
verify saved state matches reality:

- `state.app_chid` still exists (not abandoned/rewritten).
- `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s commit.
- `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` working copy.

On mismatch, refuse with a loud "state is stale — run
`vc-x1 push --restart`" error.

**Stage-prereq verification + honest completion.** Each stage
declares what it expects (working-copy dirty for commit-app;
bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both; etc.); the
dispatcher checks before running. "Completed all stages" should
only print when stages genuinely ran or were
verified-already-done, not when they were skipped without
verification.

**Implementation order:** state-sanity first (broader guard),
then per-stage prereqs (more invasive). Both should land before
the `--squash` work to avoid building on a known-fragile resume
path.

content.content:
**Shared helper:** `common::verify_tracking(repo, bookmark,
remote) -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
`jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
rather than parsing human-readable output.

## Scope design refinements (0.37.8)

Notes-only release. Captures six refinements to the scope and
squash designs ([57], [60]) from a 2026-04-23 dogfood discussion
(the manual squash workflow used to fold late changes into the
0.37.7 commit, plus follow-up Q&A on cwd independence and
plain-old-repo handling).

**Scope semantic invariant.** With `--scope=app|other|both`
(per [60]):

- `--scope=both` (or omitted; `both` is the default) → dual-repo
  operation.
- `--scope=app` → single-repo operation on the app repo (direct
  reference).
- `--scope=other` → single-repo operation on the "other" repo
  (indirect reference; resolves via `.vc-config.toml`'s
  `[workspace].other-repo` field, which already exists).

Anything that isn't `both` is single-repo. The dispatcher branches
once on this invariant; new commands inheriting `--scope` get the
semantic for free.

**`--other` is project config, not a per-command flag.** Came up
during the same discussion: should there be a `--other=PATH` flag
to override the project's "other" repo per command? Answer: no.
That belongs in `.vc-config.toml` (already there as
`[workspace].other-repo`). Keeping `--other` out of the
per-command surface keeps the flag set small and the per-command
vocabulary uniform across all commands — "other" always resolves
the same way.

**Cwd independence + counterpart-rename suggestion.** Scope names
are workspace-anchored, not cwd-relative. Each repo has its own
`.vc-config.toml` self-describing its role:

- App's config: `[workspace] path = "/" other-repo = ".claude"`.
- `.claude`'s config: `[workspace] path = "/.claude"
  other-repo = ".."`.

`path = "/"` identifies the app repo (only the app has it). The
dispatcher reads the local config, determines "am I app or other?"
via `path`, then resolves `--scope=app` and `--scope=other` to the
right physical paths regardless of cwd. From `cd .claude`:

- `--scope=app` → `..` (the app repo, found via `.claude`'s
  `other-repo`).
- `--scope=other` → `.` (`.claude` itself, since `path != "/"`
  → I AM the workspace's "other").

Naming gotcha: the config field `other-repo` means "this repo's
counterpart" (cwd-flavored direction; from `.claude` it's `..` =
the app), while CLI `--scope=other` means "the workspace's 'other'
role" (workspace-anchored; always = `.claude`). They coincide
from app's cwd (both physically = `.claude`) but diverge from
`.claude`'s cwd (config `other-repo` = `..`, CLI `--scope=other`
= `.`). Consider renaming the config field to `counterpart` or
`peer` to disambiguate from the CLI scope name.

**Plain-old-repo handling: three workspace states.** vc-x1 should
be practical without a full workspace too. Three distinguishable
states, all described by `.vc-config.toml` presence/contents:

- **POR** — no `.vc-config.toml`. Pure git/jj repo, vc-x1
  doesn't know it exists. Implicit `--scope=app`;
  `--scope=other/both` errors with "not in a vc-x1 workspace
  (no `.vc-config.toml`) — drop --scope or use --scope=app".
- **Single-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path`
  only (no `other-repo`). vc-x1-aware, no companion. Example:
  `vc-template-x1`. Implicit `--scope=app`; `--scope=other/both`
  errors with "no other-repo configured. Add `other-repo = …`
  to `.vc-config.toml` to enable dual-repo operations".
- **Dual-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path` +
  `other-repo`. Full setup. Example: `vc-x1` itself.
  `--scope=app/other/both` all valid.

Edge cases: a `.vc-config.toml` with `path != "/"` and no
`other-repo` (e.g. `path = "/.claude"`) — the repo identifies as
the "other" side but its companion is missing. Error: "config
identifies this as `/.claude` but no `other-repo` to point at the
app side; companion is missing." Dual-repo with `other-repo`
pointing somewhere that doesn't exist → loud error (workspace
state corrupted), not POR fallback.

**`vc-x1 push --squash` composition.** With `--scope` in place,
the squash design (per [57]) composes naturally:

- `vc-x1 push --squash` (no scope) → squash both repos and
  force-push both. End-to-end version of the manual two-step
  recipe in CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push".
- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=app` → squash app only.
- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=other` → squash other only.

**Dogfood validation (2026-04-23).** The manual recipe was applied
successfully to both repos in sequence to fold late changes into
the 0.37.7 commit. Observations worth carrying into the `--squash`
implementation:

- jj's `jj squash --ignore-immutable` preserves the changeID of
  the squashed-into commit. Both repos' ochid trailers stayed
  valid through the squash without any fixup. The future
  `--squash` implementation can rely on this — no ochid rewrite
  needed.
- The trimmed "Late changes after push" recipe (0.37.6, two
  lines for the `@-` case) worked identically on both sides;
  no `jj bookmark set` needed since the bookmark moves with the
  rewritten commit.
- Push state was already cleared at the end of the prior
  successful push (state reported "completed all stages (state
  cleared)"). The post-recovery `--restart` / `rm` step
  documented in CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback" wasn't
  needed in this case — but the doc remains correct for cases
  where prior push didn't clean up.

- `notes/chores-06.md` — this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry + new `[65]` reference + new
  Todo entry for the template restructure (vc-template-x1 +
  `.claude/` subdir, captured separately for later
  implementation).

## Bookmark tracking verification (0.38.0)

Cross-command tracking verification per the [63] design — every
repo-modifying command checks its target bookmark for non-tracking
remote refs and errors with the exact remediation command if any
are found. Multi-step rollout:

toolUseResult.stdout:

remote) -> Result<(), Err>` or similar. Probably use
`jj bookmark list --tracked -T <template>` under the hood
rather than parsing human-readable output.

  `other-repo`).
- `--scope=other` → `.` (`.claude` itself, since `path != "/"`
  → I AM the workspace's "other").

Naming gotcha: the config field `other-repo` means "this repo's
counterpart" (cwd-flavored direction; from `.claude` it's `..` =
the app), while CLI `--scope=other` means "the workspace's 'other'
role" (workspace-anchored; always = `.claude`). They coincide
from app's cwd (both physically = `.claude`) but diverge from
`.claude`'s cwd (config `other-repo` = `..`, CLI `--scope=other`
= `.`). Consider renaming the config field to `counterpart` or
`peer` to disambiguate from the CLI scope name.

**Plain-old-repo handling: three workspace states.** vc-x1 should
be practical without a full workspace too. Three distinguishable
states, all described by `.vc-config.toml` presence/contents:

- **POR** — no `.vc-config.toml`. Pure git/jj repo, vc-x1
  doesn't know it exists. Implicit `--scope=app`;
  `--scope=other/both` errors with "not in a vc-x1 workspace
  (no `.vc-config.toml`) — drop --scope or use --scope=app".
- **Single-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path`
  only (no `other-repo`). vc-x1-aware, no companion. Example:
  `vc-template-x1`. Implicit `--scope=app`; `--scope=other/both`
  errors with "no other-repo configured. Add `other-repo = …`
  to `.vc-config.toml` to enable dual-repo operations".
- **Dual-repo workspace** — `.vc-config.toml` with `path` +
  `other-repo`. Full setup. Example: `vc-x1` itself.
  `--scope=app/other/both` all valid.

Edge cases: a `.vc-config.toml` with `path != "/"` and no
`other-repo` (e.g. `path = "/.claude"`) — the repo identifies as
the "other" side but its companion is missing. Error: "config
identifies this as `/.claude` but no `other-repo` to point at the
app side; companion is missing." Dual-repo with `other-repo`
pointing somewhere that doesn't exist → loud error (workspace
state corrupted), not POR fallback.

**`vc-x1 push --squash` composition.** With `--scope` in place,
the squash design (per [57]) composes naturally:

- `vc-x1 push --squash` (no scope) → squash both repos and
  force-push both. End-to-end version of the manual two-step
  recipe in CLAUDE.md "Late changes after push".
- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=app` → squash app only.
- `vc-x1 push --squash --scope=other` → squash other only.

**Dogfood validation (2026-04-23).** The manual recipe was applied
successfully to both repos in sequence to fold late changes into
the 0.37.7 commit. Observations worth carrying into the `--squash`
implementation:

- jj's `jj squash --ignore-immutable` preserves the changeID of
  the squashed-into commit. Both repos' ochid trailers stayed
  valid through the squash without any fixup. The future
  `--squash` implementation can rely on this — no ochid rewrite
  needed.
- The trimmed "Late changes after push" recipe (0.37.6, two
  lines for the `@-` case) worked identically on both sides;
  no `jj bookmark set` needed since the bookmark moves with the
  rewritten commit.
- Push state was already cleared at the end of the prior
  successful push (state reported "completed all stages (state
  cleared)"). The post-recovery `--restart` / `rm` step
  documented in CLAUDE.md "Manual finalize fallback" wasn't
  needed in this case — but the doc remains correct for cases
  where prior push didn't clean up.

- `notes/chores-06.md` — this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry + new `[65]` reference + new
  Todo entry for the template restructure (vc-template-x1 +
  `.claude/` subdir, captured separately for later
  implementation).

Cross-command tracking verification per the [63] design — every
repo-modifying command checks its target bookmark for non-tracking
remote refs and errors with the exact remediation command if any
are found. Multi-step rollout:

### 0.41.0-3: capture --scope-everywhere direction

Notes-only step. Records the directional decisions that
came out of the 0.41.0-2 close-out conversation
(2026-04-26) so the rest of the cycle and the post-0.41.0
follow-ups don't relitigate them.

**Consistency principle (decided).** Every subcommand that
operates on one or both repos gets `--scope=code|bot|code,
bot`. No vocabulary forks across commands; `--scope` is the
project-wide convention. Existing per-command flags
(`-R/--repo`, `--repo`) stay as back-compat aliases where
they already exist; new commands inherit `--scope` from day
one.

**Per-command rollout map.**

- `init` — done (0.40.0). `--scope=code|bot|code,bot`.
- `sync` — done (0.41.0-2). `--scope` + `-R` (mutually
  exclusive); `-R` retained for arbitrary multi-repo.
- `push` — 0.41.0-4. State machine becomes scope-aware;
  single-side path skips `commit-claude` /
  `finalize-claude` and bookmarks one repo only.
- `finalize` — 0.41.0-5. `--scope` is canonical; existing
  `--repo` becomes a back-compat alias (overrides scope
  when present, prints a soft deprecation note, slated
  for removal in a future cycle). Does *not* downgrade to
  the "review-only / no-op" outcome the original 0.41.0-4
  sketch hedged toward.
- `clone` — post-0.41.0. Parallel to `init --scope`;
  single-repo clone for `vc-template-x1`-shape remotes.
- `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` — post-0.41.0. Same
  vocabulary as everywhere else, no rename to `--side`.
  Semantic: `--scope=code` validates code's commits
  against bot, `--scope=bot` reverses the direction,
  `--scope=code,bot` does both (the new default).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — post-0.41.0. Add
  `--scope` via `CommonArgs` so all four inherit it
  in one cycle. Existing `-R` / `--repo` stays alongside.
- `symlink` — N/A. Bot-only by definition; scope doesn't
  apply.

**Deferred design questions** (caught in todo, not blocking
the rollout):

- `[workspace]` section name in `.vc-config.toml` reads as
  a Cargo workspace to Rust users but isn't one. Rename
  candidates: `[repo-list]` / `[project]` / `[dual-repo]`.
  Breaking change; needs migration.
- Help layout uniformity — clap auto-picks two-column vs
  over-under per command based on widest flag spec.
  `sync -h` is two-column, `init -h` is over-under. Force
  uniform via `next_line_help(true)` at the root.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-3`.
- `CLAUDE.md`: `### Versioning` cross-references the new
  In Progress dev-ladder convention.
- `notes/chores-06.md`: refine the [71] cycle-step sketch
  to insert this `-3` notes step and renumber push (was
  `-3`) and finalize (was `-4`); add this subsection.
- `notes/todo.md`: three new Todo entries — `clone`
  `--scope`, `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` `--scope`,
  `CommonArgs` (`chid`/`desc`/`list`/`show`) `--scope`.
  In Progress reshaped — explanatory intro paragraph
  shows the dev-ladder format, and the active entry is
  rewritten as the bulleted ladder with
  `(done)` / `(current)` markers.

### 0.41.0-4: capture --scope enum vocabulary

Notes-only step. Mid-implementation rethink — the
original `-4` (push --scope on top of `Vec<Side>`)
exposed a vocabulary gap that the user surfaced
2026-04-26: `code` plays two distinct roles depending on
context (a *role* in a dual workspace; an implicit
*fallback to cwd* in POR). Conflating them muddies what
"obvious to the user" looks like. Splitting them honestly
is a type-model change, not a per-command implementation
detail — so the cycle pivots: capture the refined
vocabulary here, defer all command implementations to
0.42.0, and close 0.41.0 cleanly.

**Settled vocabulary.**

- `--scope` (short form `-s`) accepts:
  - `code` — the app side of a dual-repo workspace.
  - `bot` — the bot side of a dual-repo workspace.
  - `code,bot` / `bot,code` — both (commutative; same
    semantic, same default).
  - `<path>` — single-repo mode; ignore `.vc-config.toml`,
    operate on the given path. Path values must be
    prefixed (`./`, `/`, `~/`) to disambiguate from the
    keyword set. Tilde expansion mirrors init's existing
    behavior on `--repo-local` / `--repo-remote`.
- No discoverable `single` keyword. `--scope=.` is the
  cwd form; `--scope=/abs/path` or `--scope=./rel` is the
  explicit form. Bare names that aren't keywords error
  with a "did you mean `./foo`?" hint.

**Internal type model.**

```rust
pub enum Scope {
    Roles(Vec<Side>), // code | bot | code,bot
    Single(PathBuf),  // explicit path, single-repo mode
}
```

Replaces today's `Scope(pub Vec<Side>)`. The current
`has_code` / `has_bot` / `is_code_only` etc. helpers all
shift to operate on `Roles` only — `Single(_)` is a
distinct mode, not a one-element role list.

**Default-scope rules (revised).**

| Workspace state | `.vc-config.toml` shape | Default scope |
|---|---|---|
| Dual workspace | `path` + non-empty `other-repo` | `Roles([Code, Bot])` |
| Single-repo workspace | `path`, no `other-repo` | `Roles([Code])` |
| POR | (no file) | `Single(cwd)` |

The single-repo-workspace concept stays — it's a real,
distinct state with a non-trivial default (`Roles([Code])`
not `Single(...)`). `--scope=bot` errors there with
"no other-repo configured."

**Per-command applicability matrix.**

| Command | `Roles` accepted | `Single(_)` accepted |
|---|---|---|
| `init` | yes | no — init creates workspaces, not POR repos |
| `sync` | yes | yes |
| `push` | yes | yes |
| `finalize` | yes (currently has `--repo`; migrates to `--scope`) | yes |
| `clone` | yes | yes (single-repo clone target) |
| `validate-desc` | yes | **error** — validate compares two repos by definition; `Single` is meaningless |
| `fix-desc` | yes | **error** (same reason as validate-desc) |
| `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` | yes | yes |
| `symlink` | n/a | n/a |

`Single` is *always* available as the explicit
"ignore-the-config" escape hatch on commands where it
applies, even inside a dual workspace — the user
explicitly bypasses the dispatcher. On commands where it
can't apply (validate-desc / fix-desc), errors with a
specific message rather than silently doing the wrong
thing.

**Flag-surface changes.**

- Drop `-R` / `--repo` everywhere. Currently sync has it
  (mutually exclusive with `--scope`); finalize has its
  own `--repo` (single repo). Both fold into the new
  `--scope=<path>` form.
- `--scope` gets short form `-s` for ergonomics.
- The path form covers what `-R` / `--repo` did before.
  Multi-repo (sync's old `-R . -R .claude` style) is
  deliberately not preserved — that capability had no
  real users beyond manually emulating today's default.

**Rationale for the cycle pivot.**

- The 0.41.0-3 capture committed to "consistency
  everywhere", which means push, finalize, and the
  remaining commands all need to agree on the type
  model. Implementing push on the soon-to-be-changed
  type would be churn.
- The enum refactor touches scope.rs, common.rs,
  init, sync, plus push and finalize at the same time.
  That's a 0.42.0-sized cycle, not a sub-step inside
  0.41.0.
- Closing 0.41.0 here produces a clean checkpoint: the
  scope foundation (0.40.0 init) and one consumer
  (0.41.0-2 sync) are shipped, the direction is captured
  in 0.41.0-3 and 0.41.0-4. 0.42.0 opens with the type
  refactor as its `-0` plan.

Edits:

- `Cargo.toml`: bump to `0.41.0-4` (notes-only step).
- `src/push.rs`: revert the partial scope work that was
  in flight (`--scope` flag on `PushArgs`, scope-related
  imports). Push stays unchanged on main; its
  scope wiring lands in 0.42.0 against the new type.
- `notes/chores-06.md`: cycle-step sketch updated (0.41.0-3
  marked shipped; 0.41.0-4 redefined as the enum
  capture; 0.41.0-5 dropped from the cycle); this
  subsection added; legacy default-scope rules marked
  superseded.
- `notes/todo.md`: dev-ladder updated (0.41.0-3 → done;
  0.41.0-4 current; 0.41.0-5 removed; 0.41.0 close-out
  noted).

### 0.41.0: cycle close-out

126	pub struct PushArgs {
127	    /// Bookmark to advance in both repos (positional form of `--bookmark`).
128	    ///
129	    /// Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
130	    /// without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an
131	    /// alias for scripts and for composition with other args. The two
132	    /// forms conflict if both supplied.
133	    #[arg(value_name = "BOOKMARK", conflicts_with = "bookmark")]
134	    pub bookmark_pos: Option<String>,
135	
136	    /// Bookmark to advance in both repos (flag form; see positional).
137	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "bookmark_pos")]
138	    pub bookmark: Option<String>,
139	
140	    /// Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1.
141	    #[arg(long)]
142	    pub restart: bool,
143	
144	    /// Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use).
145	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STAGE")]
146	    pub from: Option<Stage>,
147	
148	    /// Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate.
149	    #[arg(long)]
150	    pub step: bool,
151	
152	    /// Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit.
153	    #[arg(long)]
154	    pub status: bool,
155	
156	    /// Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run succeeded).
157	    #[arg(long)]
158	    pub recheck: bool,
159	
160	    /// Stop before `finalize-claude` so it can be run manually.
161	    #[arg(long)]
162	    pub no_finalize: bool,
163	
164	    /// Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects.
165	    #[arg(long)]
166	    pub dry_run: bool,
167	
168	    /// Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage).
169	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STR")]
170	    pub title: Option<String>,
171	
172	    /// Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage).
173	    #[arg(long, value_name = "STR")]
174	    pub body: Option<String>,
175	
176	    /// Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
177	    /// confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both
178	    /// supplied in non-interactive contexts.
179	    #[arg(short = 'y', long)]
180	    pub yes: bool,
181	}
182	
183	/// Current state-file format version — bump when the flat key set
184	/// changes incompatibly so readers can detect stale state and refuse
185	/// to resume instead of silently misinterpreting fields.
186	const STATE_FORMAT_VERSION: u32 = 1;
187	
188	/// Default directory (relative to app-repo root) for the push state
189	/// file when `.vc-config.toml` has no `[push]` override.
190	const DEFAULT_STATE_DIR: &str = ".vc-x1";
191	
192	/// Default filename for the push state file under `state_dir`.
193	const DEFAULT_STATE_FILE: &str = "push-state.toml";
194	
195	/// Resolved state-file layout — just the full path today, structured
196	/// so future additions (e.g. a `dir` exposed for `.gitignore`
197	/// coherence checks in 0.37.0-3) have a place to land without
198	/// changing callers.
199	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
200	pub struct StateLayout {
201	    /// Full path `<repo>/<state-dir>/<state-file>`.
202	    pub path: PathBuf,
203	}
204	
205	/// Read `state_dir` / `state_file` from `<repo_root>/.vc-config.toml`
206	/// under the `[push]` section, falling back to defaults (`.vc-x1`
207	/// and `push-state.toml`).
208	///
209	/// Missing config file is not an error — every workspace has the
210	/// defaults applied silently so `vc-x1 push --status` works on a
211	/// fresh clone without ceremony.
212	pub fn resolve_state_layout(repo_root: &Path) -> StateLayout {
213	    let config_path = repo_root.join(".vc-config.toml");
214	    let (dir, file) = if config_path.exists() {
215	        match toml_load(&config_path) {
216	            Ok(map) => {
217	                let dir = map
218	                    .get("push.state-dir")
219	                    .cloned()
220	                    .unwrap_or_else(|| DEFAULT_STATE_DIR.to_string());
221	                let file = map
222	                    .get("push.state-file")
223	                    .cloned()
224	                    .unwrap_or_else(|| DEFAULT_STATE_FILE.to_string());
225	                (dir, file)
226	            }
227	            Err(e) => {
228	                debug!("push: ignoring unparseable .vc-config.toml: {e}");
229	                (
230	                    DEFAULT_STATE_DIR.to_string(),
231	                    DEFAULT_STATE_FILE.to_string(),
232	                )
233	            }
234	        }
235	    } else {
236	        (
237	            DEFAULT_STATE_DIR.to_string(),
238	            DEFAULT_STATE_FILE.to_string(),
239	        )
240	    };
241	    let path = repo_root.join(&dir).join(&file);
242	    StateLayout { path }
243	}
244	
245	/// Persistent state for an in-progress `push` run.
246	///
247	/// Serialized as flat TOML (`key = "value"`) under the dir/file
248	/// configured in `.vc-config.toml`'s `[push]` section. Fields added
249	/// across dev steps are all `Option<_>` so older states remain
250	/// loadable — only `version` / `stage` / `bookmark` / `started_at`
251	/// are required. See the module docstring for which dev step
252	/// introduced which field.
253	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
254	pub struct PushState {
255	    /// State-file format version; must match `STATE_FORMAT_VERSION`
256	    /// to be considered readable.
257	    pub version: u32,
258	    /// The next stage to execute on resume.
259	    pub stage: Stage,
260	    /// Bookmark being advanced by this run (persisted so resume
261	    /// doesn't need `--bookmark` again).
262	    pub bookmark: String,
263	    /// ISO-8601 UTC timestamp captured when the state was first
264	    /// written. Informational — helps the user spot stale state.
265	    pub started_at: String,
266	    /// App-repo changeID captured at `message` stage (before
267	    /// `commit-app` runs). Stable across `jj commit` — becomes the
268	    /// chid of the just-committed change. Used when composing the
269	    /// `.claude` commit's ochid trailer. Added in 0.37.0-2.
270	    pub app_chid: Option<String>,
271	    /// `.claude` repo changeID used by the app-repo commit's ochid
272	    /// trailer. Either the pre-commit `@` chid (when `.claude` has
273	    /// pending changes — becomes `@-` after commit) or the current
274	    /// `@-` chid (when `.claude` is clean — stays stable). Added in
275	    /// 0.37.0-2.
276	    pub claude_chid: Option<String>,
277	    /// Whether `.claude`'s working copy had changes at `message`
278	    /// time. Decides whether `commit-claude` actually runs or
279	    /// skips. Added in 0.37.0-2.
280	    pub claude_had_changes: Option<bool>,
281	    /// `jj op` id of the app repo captured before `commit-app`. On
282	    /// failure in stages 4-6, `jj op restore` rewinds here. Added
283	    /// in 0.37.0-2.
284	    pub op_app: Option<String>,
285	    /// `jj op` id of `.claude` captured before `commit-app`. Same
286	    /// rollback target as `op_app`. Added in 0.37.0-2.
287	    pub op_claude: Option<String>,
288	    /// Composed commit title — persisted so resume doesn't need
289	    /// `--title` re-passed. Set during `message` stage from either
290	    /// `--title` or `$EDITOR`. Added in 0.37.0-4.
291	    pub title: Option<String>,
292	    /// Composed commit body (sans ochid trailer, which each commit
293	    /// stage appends). Persisted alongside title. Multi-line
294	    /// content is escaped for the flat-TOML save format (see
295	    /// `escape_multiline` / `unescape_multiline`). Added in
296	    /// 0.37.0-4.
297	    pub body: Option<String>,
298	}
299	
300	impl PushState {
301	    /// Build a fresh state for a new run.
302	    pub fn new_for(bookmark: &str) -> Self {
303	        PushState {
304	            version: STATE_FORMAT_VERSION,
305	            stage: Stage::first(),
306	            bookmark: bookmark.to_string(),
307	            started_at: Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
308	            app_chid: None,
309	            claude_chid: None,
310	            claude_had_changes: None,
311	            op_app: None,
312	            op_claude: None,
313	            title: None,
314	            body: None,
315	        }
316	    }
317	
318	    /// Write the state to `path`, creating parent dirs as needed.
319	    ///
320	    /// Values are single-line and contain no `"` characters under
321	    /// normal operation (stage names are kebab-case, chids are
322	    /// ASCII, bookmark names don't contain quotes, timestamps are
323	    /// ASCII). A defensive escape pass replaces any stray `"` with
324	    /// `\"` so the file always parses with `toml_simple`. Optional
325	    /// fields are only emitted when set so older state files don't
326	    /// carry a wall of blank keys.
327	    pub fn save(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
328	        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {
329	            fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;
330	        }
331	        let mut content = String::new();
332	        content.push_str("# vc-x1 push state — managed file, do not edit by hand\n");
333	        content.push_str("[push-state]\n");
334	        content.push_str(&format!("version = {}\n", self.version));
335	        content.push_str(&format!("stage = \"{}\"\n", self.stage.as_str()));
336	        content.push_str(&format!("bookmark = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(&self.bookmark)));
337	        content.push_str(&format!(
338	            "started_at = \"{}\"\n",
339	            escape_toml(&self.started_at)
340	        ));
341	        if let Some(v) = &self.app_chid {
342	            content.push_str(&format!("app_chid = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(v)));
343	        }
344	        if let Some(v) = &self.claude_chid {
345	            content.push_str(&format!("claude_chid = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(v)));
346	        }
347	        if let Some(v) = self.claude_had_changes {
348	            content.push_str(&format!("claude_had_changes = {v}\n"));
349	        }
350	        if let Some(v) = &self.op_app {
351	            content.push_str(&format!("op_app = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(v)));
352	        }
353	        if let Some(v) = &self.op_claude {
354	            content.push_str(&format!("op_claude = \"{}\"\n", escape_toml(v)));
355	        }

1340	/// Verify the world matches the saved state after all stages have run.
1341	///
1342	/// Counterpart to `verify_state_sanity` (which runs pre-stage). This
1343	/// runs after the stage loop completes and confirms the post-conditions
1344	/// are real — directly addresses the 0.37.1 false-success symptom
1345	/// (push declared "completed all stages" while WC still held
1346	/// uncommitted changes from a stale-state resume).
1347	///
1348	/// Three checks:
1349	///
1350	/// 1. App's bookmark is at a commit whose chid matches `state.app_chid`.
1351	/// 2. App's working copy has no uncommitted changes (commit-app should
1352	///    have captured anything that was there).
1353	/// 3. `.claude`'s bookmark is at `state.claude_chid`'s commit (when set).
1354	///    `.claude`'s WC may legitimately have new session writes from the
1355	///    push run itself — finalize-claude (detached) handles those — so
1356	///    no WC-clean check on `.claude`.
1357	///
1358	/// On any mismatch: returns `Err`. The caller surfaces as a loud
1359	/// warning rather than blocking — push has already crossed the remote
1360	/// boundary, work is live on origin, the warning prompts the user to
1361	/// investigate.
1362	fn verify_completion_sanity(
1363	    root: &Path,
1364	    state: &PushState,
1365	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
1366	    let bookmark = &state.bookmark;
1367	    let root_str = root.to_string_lossy();
1368	    let cwd = Path::new(".");
1369	
1370	    // 1. app bookmark at state.app_chid.
1371	    if let Some(app_chid) = &state.app_chid {
1372	        let actual = run(
1373	            "jj",
1374	            &[
1375	                "log",
1376	                "-r",
1377	                bookmark,
1378	                "--no-graph",
1379	                "-T",
1380	                "change_id.short(12)",
1381	                "-R",
1382	                &root_str,
1383	            ],
1384	            cwd,
1385	        )?;
1386	        let actual = actual.trim();
1387	        if actual != app_chid.as_str() {
1388	            return Err(format!(
1389	                "completion sanity: app bookmark '{bookmark}' is at chid '{actual}' but \
1390	                 state.app_chid is '{app_chid}'."
1391	            )
1392	            .into());
1393	        }
1394	    }
1395	
1396	    // 2. app WC clean (no uncommitted changes). Use the `empty`
1397	    // template — `jj diff --stat` always emits a "0 files changed"
1398	    // line even when clean, so plain output-emptiness check would
1399	    // false-positive.
1400	    if !jj_log_empty(root, "@")? {
1401	        let wc_diff = run("jj", &["diff", "--stat", "-r", "@", "-R", &root_str], cwd)?;
1402	        return Err(format!(
1403	            "completion sanity: app working copy has uncommitted changes (push completed \
1404	             but WC dirty?). Diff stat:\n{wc_diff}"
1405	        )
1406	        .into());
1407	    }
1408	
1409	    // 3. .claude bookmark at state.claude_chid.
1410	    if let Some(claude_chid) = &state.claude_chid {
1411	        let claude = claude_path(root);
1412	        let claude_str = claude.to_string_lossy();
1413	        let actual = run(
1414	            "jj",
1415	            &[
1416	                "log",
1417	                "-r",
1418	                bookmark,
1419	                "--no-graph",
1420	                "-T",
1421	                "change_id.short(12)",
1422	                "-R",
1423	                &claude_str,
1424	            ],
1425	            cwd,
1426	        )?;
1427	        let actual = actual.trim();
1428	        if actual != claude_chid.as_str() {
1429	            return Err(format!(
1430	                "completion sanity: .claude bookmark '{bookmark}' is at chid '{actual}' but \
1431	                 state.claude_chid is '{claude_chid}'."
1432	            )
1433	            .into());
1434	        }
1435	    }
1436	
1437	    Ok(())
1438	}

12	/// Fetch and sync a set of repos to their remotes.
13	///
14	/// Default is `--check`: fetch + classify + report, **error** if any
15	/// repo is `behind` or `diverged`. Pass `--no-check` to actually
16	/// rebase / fast-forward. On any failure the starting state of every
17	/// repo is restored via `jj op restore`.
18	///
19	/// Scripts and automation should pass `--check` or `--no-check`
20	/// explicitly rather than relying on the default — defaults can shift,
21	/// explicit flags lock in the contract.
22	///
23	/// Repo set is resolved from `--scope`:
24	///
25	/// - Explicit `--scope=code|bot|code,bot|<path>` — keyword forms
26	///   resolve via the project root's `.vc-config.toml`; the path
27	///   form syncs that one repo (single-repo mode).
28	/// - No `--scope` — workspace-default scope:
29	///   - dual workspace (`.vc-config.toml` with `other-repo`) → `code,bot`
30	///   - single-repo workspace (`.vc-config.toml`, no `other-repo`) → `code`
31	///   - POR (no `.vc-config.toml`) → cwd as a single-repo target
32	#[derive(Args, Debug)]
33	pub struct SyncArgs {
34	    /// Verify only — fetch + classify; error if any repo needs action.
35	    /// This is the default; pass explicitly in scripts.
36	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "no_check")]
37	    pub check: bool,
38	
39	    /// Apply — fetch + classify, then rebase / fast-forward as needed.
40	    #[arg(long, conflicts_with = "check")]
41	    pub no_check: bool,
42	
43	    /// Suppress all informational output (exit code signals result)
44	    #[arg(short, long)]
45	    pub quiet: bool,
46	
47	    /// Bookmark to sync in each repo
48	    #[arg(long, default_value = "main")]
49	    pub bookmark: String,
50	
51	    /// Remote to sync against
52	    #[arg(long, default_value = "origin")]
53	    pub remote: String,
54	
55	    /// Which repo(s) of the workspace to sync.
56	    ///
57	    /// `SCOPE=code|bot|code,bot|<path>`:
58	    ///
59	    /// - `code` — sync only the app repo.
60	    /// - `bot` — sync only the bot repo (errors if no bot repo
61	    ///   is configured).
62	    /// - `code,bot` — sync both repos.
63	    /// - `<path>` (`./X`, `/X`, `~/X`) — single-repo mode; sync
64	    ///   only that repo, ignoring `.vc-config.toml`.
65	    ///
66	    /// Default depends on workspace state: dual workspace →
67	    /// `code,bot`; single-repo workspace → `code`; POR → cwd.
68	    #[arg(
69	        short = 's',
70	        long,
71	        value_name = "SCOPE",
72	        value_parser = parse_scope,
73	        verbatim_doc_comment
74	    )]
75	    pub scope: Option<Scope>,
76	}
77	
78	/// Resolve `args` into the concrete repo list `sync_repos` operates on.
79	///
80	/// Explicit `--scope` wins; otherwise resolves via `default_scope`
81	/// against the discovered workspace root and cwd.
82	fn resolve_args_to_repos(args: &SyncArgs) -> Result<Vec<PathBuf>, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
83	    let workspace_root = find_workspace_root();
84	    let cwd = std::env::current_dir()?;
85	    let scope = match &args.scope {
86	        Some(s) => s.clone(),
87	        None => default_scope(workspace_root.as_deref(), &cwd),
88	    };
89	    scope_to_repos(&scope, workspace_root.as_deref())
90	}

95	    /// Local and remote point at the same commit.
96	    UpToDate,
97	    /// Local is a strict ancestor of remote — fast-forward possible.
98	    Behind { local: String, remote: String },
99	    /// Remote is a strict ancestor of local — nothing to pull in.
100	    Ahead { local: String, remote: String },
101	    /// Neither is an ancestor of the other — needs rebase.
102	    Diverged { local: String, remote: String },
103	    /// The bookmark has no `@<remote>` counterpart.
104	    NoRemote,
105	}

37	/// Named stages of the `push` state machine.
38	///
39	/// Used by `--from <stage>` to resume at a specific point and by
40	/// `--status` to report the current position. Ordered top-down so
41	/// `Stage as u8` comparisons reflect progress through the flow.
42	#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, ValueEnum)]
43	#[value(rename_all = "kebab-case")]
44	pub enum Stage {
45	    /// Run fmt / clippy / test / install / retest.
46	    Preflight,
47	    /// Present diff for the first approval gate.
48	    Review,
49	    /// Compose / edit the commit message; present for second gate.
50	    Message,
51	    /// Commit the app repo.
52	    CommitApp,
53	    /// Commit the `.claude` session repo (skipped if empty).
54	    CommitClaude,
55	    /// Advance both bookmarks to `@-`.
56	    BookmarkBoth,
57	    /// `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`.
58	    PushApp,
59	    /// `vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --squash --push <b> ...`.
60	    FinalizeClaude,
61	}
62	
63	impl Stage {
64	    /// Return the stage's kebab-case string identifier (matches what
65	    /// the CLI accepts via `--from <stage>` and what `PushState`
66	    /// persists to disk).
67	    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
68	        match self {
69	            Stage::Preflight => "preflight",
70	            Stage::Review => "review",
71	            Stage::Message => "message",
72	            Stage::CommitApp => "commit-app",
73	            Stage::CommitClaude => "commit-claude",
74	            Stage::BookmarkBoth => "bookmark-both",
75	            Stage::PushApp => "push-app",
76	            Stage::FinalizeClaude => "finalize-claude",
77	        }
78	    }
79	
80	    /// Parse a kebab-case stage name back into a `Stage`.
81	    ///
82	    /// Unknown names return `None`; callers should surface a helpful
83	    /// error rather than silently substituting a default.
84	    pub fn from_str(name: &str) -> Option<Self> {
85	        match name {
86	            "preflight" => Some(Stage::Preflight),
87	            "review" => Some(Stage::Review),
88	            "message" => Some(Stage::Message),
89	            "commit-app" => Some(Stage::CommitApp),
90	            "commit-claude" => Some(Stage::CommitClaude),
91	            "bookmark-both" => Some(Stage::BookmarkBoth),
92	            "push-app" => Some(Stage::PushApp),
93	            "finalize-claude" => Some(Stage::FinalizeClaude),
94	            _ => None,
95	        }
96	    }
97	
98	    /// The stage that follows this one, or `None` when this is the
99	    /// last stage (`FinalizeClaude`).
100	    pub fn next(self) -> Option<Self> {
101	        match self {
102	            Stage::Preflight => Some(Stage::Review),
103	            Stage::Review => Some(Stage::Message),
104	            Stage::Message => Some(Stage::CommitApp),
105	            Stage::CommitApp => Some(Stage::CommitClaude),
106	            Stage::CommitClaude => Some(Stage::BookmarkBoth),
107	            Stage::BookmarkBoth => Some(Stage::PushApp),
108	            Stage::PushApp => Some(Stage::FinalizeClaude),
109	            Stage::FinalizeClaude => None,
110	        }
111	    }
112	
113	    /// The first stage in the flow — used when no saved state exists.
114	    pub fn first() -> Self {
115	        Stage::Preflight
116	    }

I have the full picture. Per CLAUDE.md I'll propose the plan first and pause for approval before touching code.

---

## Plan: 0.42.0-4 — `push --scope`

**Goal.** Make push's state machine scope-aware on top of the new `Scope` enum. Add `--scope` / `-s` to `PushArgs`, persist scope in `PushState` so resumes can't silently switch shape, and have each stage run or no-op based on scope.

**Scope semantics for push.**

| Scope | Runs |
|---|---|
| `Roles([Code, Bot])` (today's default in dual workspaces) | all 8 stages, ochid trailers on both commits |
| `Roles([Code])` | preflight (code only), review (code), message (code chid only), commit-app (no ochid), bookmark code, push code, **skip** commit-claude / finalize-claude |
| `Roles([Bot])` | preflight (bot only), review (bot), message (bot chid only), commit-bot, bookmark bot, push bot, **skip** commit-app / push-app / finalize |
| `Single(<path>)` | preflight (path), review (path), message (path chid), commit-path, bookmark path, push path, **skip** finalize |

Single-side and `Single` modes drop the `ochid:` trailer (no counterpart commit to point at).

**Edits.**

- `Cargo.toml` — `0.42.0-3` → `0.42.0-4`.
- `src/push.rs`:
  - `PushArgs`: add `scope: Option<Scope>` (short `-s`, `value_parser = parse_scope`, `verbatim_doc_comment`).
  - `PushState`: add `scope: Option<Scope>` (serialized to TOML — keyword form for `Roles`, path string for `Single`); on resume, if `--scope` differs, error "scope mismatch — `--restart`".
  - Bump `STATE_FORMAT_VERSION` to `2`; v1 state files refuse to load with the same "`--restart`" remediation.
  - Introduce `resolve_repos(workspace_root, scope) -> Vec<PathBuf>` (small wrapper over `scope_to_repos`) and thread a resolved `(code_path?, bot_path?)` through the stages.
  - Each stage gets a scope guard. The stage names stay as-is (`commit-claude`, `bookmark-both`, `finalize-claude`) — not renaming this cycle keeps the diff small and the state-format rationale singular (resume safety, not naming churn).
  - `verify_state_sanity` / `verify_completion_sanity`: only check the chids/WC that the active scope produced.
- Integration tests in `src/push.rs`: cover `Roles([Code])` and one `Single(_)` shape end-to-end against the existing `Fixture`. (`Roles([Bot])` if it falls out cheaply — can defer to dogfood otherwise.)
- `notes/todo.md` — flip `-3` to `(done)`, `-4` to `(current)`.
- `notes/chores-07.md` — add `### 0.42.0-4: push --scope` post-impl subsection.

**Open design questions before I start.**

1. **Bot-only finalize.** `finalize-claude` exists to detach a squash for *trailing* session writes after foreground push exits. On `Roles([Bot])` push, push *is* the bot operation — no trailing writes from another command. **Recommend:** skip `finalize-claude` on bot-only too. Agree, or want the detached squash to still run "for symmetry"?

2. **Stage renames.** `commit-claude` / `bookmark-both` / `finalize-claude` read awkwardly under `code|bot` vocab and `Single` mode. **Recommend:** leave them this cycle — renaming would compound the state-format bump and inflate the diff. A naming sweep can be its own future step. OK to defer?

3. **State format bump (v1 → v2).** Recommend loud refusal of v1 state with `--restart` remediation rather than back-fill `scope = "code,bot"`. Worst-case impact: anyone with a parked push-state.toml gets one prompt to restart. Acceptable?

Awaiting your read on the three questions (or any other shape changes you'd want) before I touch code.

message.content: Question, I'm surprized we need to do these "basic" operations, I would have thought these were already done when sync got --scope capabilities?

content.text:
Good catch — fair question. The earlier sub-steps deliberately stayed minimal:

- **0.42.0-1** added the enum + helpers (no I/O).
- **0.42.0-2** added `parse_scope` (input direction only).
- **0.42.0-3** wired `sync` to `--scope`, but sync is one-shot — resolve scope at startup, run, exit. Nothing to serialize.

Push is the first consumer that **persists** scope, because the state machine resumes across invocations. If a user runs `vc-x1 push main --scope=code`, leaves, then comes back and runs bare `vc-x1 push`, the resume must know to stay code-only — otherwise it silently expands to the workspace default `code,bot` and clobbers the bot side. So push needs to round-trip `Scope` through `push-state.toml`, and that needs a stable string form (`Display` + `parse_scope` as inverses).

That's why this is hitting now and not in -3.

That said — there are other shapes if you'd rather:

1. **`Display` impl on `Scope`** (what I started). Symmetric with `parse_scope`; lets any future persister (cron-state, undo-anchor, etc.) reuse it.
2. **Push-private `scope_to_state_str` / `state_str_to_scope` helpers.** Keeps `scope.rs` purely about parsing/matching; serialization stays in push. Smaller blast radius if we ever want a different on-disk encoding for some future consumer.
3. **Persist the resolved paths (`Vec<String>`) instead of the expression.** Avoids the serialization layer entirely. But loses the intent — `code,bot` and `["./", "./.claude"]` resolve identically but mean different things at the matcher level (e.g., ochid-trailer logic looks at "are both sides in scope?", which is a `Scope` shape question, not a path-list question).

I leaned to (1) because it's the smallest surface change and matches `parse_scope`'s existing role. Does that read right to you, or want option (2) so this stays push-internal?

message.content: hmm, does sync support --scope=<path>?

content.text:
Yes — sync supports it via the `Single(_)` variant. From -3's smoke tests:

```
vc-x1 sync --check -s ./.claude → 1 repo (Single-mode resolves directly to that path)
vc-x1 sync --check -s code      → 1 repo (Roles via .vc-config.toml)
cd /tmp && vc-x1 sync --check    → POR detected, default Single(cwd)
```

But sync only exercises **parse → resolve → run**. It never serializes a `Scope` back out, so `Display` was never needed. The serialization gap is the part that's specifically push (and any other future stateful command).

So `parse_scope` + `scope_to_repos` are reused as-is in push — those *are* already done. The only "basic" addition is the inverse direction (`Scope → string`) for the state file. That's genuinely new because nothing before push needed to write a `Scope` back to disk.

Want me to keep `Display` on `Scope` (option 1) or hide the serialization in push as private helpers (option 2)?

lastPrompt: hmm, does sync support --scope=<path>?

message.content: Sorry, what is the definition of serialize in this context

content.text:
In this context, "serialize" just means **convert the in-memory `Scope` value to a string we can write to disk**, and "deserialize" means the inverse — read that string back and reconstruct the `Scope`.

Concrete example. After someone runs `vc-x1 push main --scope=code`, push's state file `.vc-x1/push-state.toml` looks like this today:

```toml
[push-state]
version = 1
stage = "commit-app"
bookmark = "main"
started_at = "2026-04-27T15:30:00Z"
app_chid = "abc123def456"
title = "feat: x"
body = "..."
```

To make resume scope-aware, I need to add one more line:

```toml
scope = "code"
```

That `"code"` string is the **serialized** form of the in-memory `Scope::Roles(vec![Side::Code])` value. When push resumes later, it reads the file, sees `scope = "code"`, and **deserializes** by calling `parse_scope("code")` to rebuild `Scope::Roles(vec![Side::Code])`.

The two directions:

- `Scope` → `String` (serialize): `format!("{}", scope)` if I add `Display`, or a private `scope_to_state_str(&scope)` helper.
- `String` → `Scope` (deserialize): `parse_scope(s)` — already exists.

So the question I asked earlier is just: where should the serialize half live?

- **Option 1:** `impl Display for Scope` in `scope.rs` — alongside `parse_scope`, paired symmetrically.
- **Option 2:** A private `scope_to_state_str` function inside `push.rs` — keeps `scope.rs` purely about parsing, and any other future persister would write its own.

Both produce the exact same on-disk string `"code"` / `"bot"` / `"code,bot"` / `"./mypath"`. The choice is just about where the function lives.

There's no code path that would treat `--scope=<path>` differently between modes.

But you're right that **empirically** -3 only smoke-tested `--check`. We haven't actually run `vc-x1 sync --no-check -s ./somepath` to confirm it works end-to-end. The reasoning is "the code paths are uniform so it should," not "we proved it."

content.content:
vc-x1 0.42.0-4

Create a new dual-repo project

Usage: vc-x1 init [OPTIONS] [NAME]

Arguments:
  [NAME]
          Project name (directory and GitHub repo name).
          
          - Optional with `--repo-remote` (derived from URL's last
            path segment, trailing `.git` stripped).
          - Required with `--repo-local` and in default mode.
          - Fatal if both given and they disagree.

Options:
      --owner <OWNER>
          GitHub user or organization.
          
          - Only meaningful in default mode.
          - Fatal with `--repo-local` or `--repo-remote`.

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --dir <DIR>
          Parent directory to create the project under [default: cwd].
          
          - Fatal with `--repo-local` (that flag supplies the parent).

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --private
          Create private GitHub repos (default: public).
          
          - Only meaningful in default mode.
          - Fatal with `--repo-local` or `--repo-remote`.

      --dry-run
          Dry run — show what would be done without executing

      --push-retries <PUSH_RETRIES>
          Max push retries after repo creation [default: 5]
          
          [default: 5]

      --push-retry-delay <PUSH_RETRY_DELAY>
          Seconds between push retries [default: 3]
          
          [default: 3]

      --use-template <CODE[,BOT]>
          Seed repos from template directories.
          
          Value is `CODE[,BOT]`. Default bot path is `<CODE>.claude`
          (file-name concat, not path join — templates are siblings).
          
          - With `--scope=code`: only `CODE` is used; passing `,BOT`
            is fatal (no session side to seed).
